name: Gateau Rouge Diable
recipeIngredient: 4 tsp artificial sweetener (sugar twin is one that remains sweet after cooking)
recipeInstructions:
1. Place raisins in a small bowl and pour over 1/2 cup boiling water. Set aside to steep for 20 minutes.
2. In a large bowl, blend cocoa with 3/4 cup boiling water. Set aside to cool.
3. Preheat oven to 350°F (175°C). Lightly coat a 9-inch cake pan with non-stick spray and dust with 1 or 2 tablespoons flour, coating completely.
4. Over a medium bowl, sift together flour, baking soda, and salt.
5. Add raisins and boiling water to the beaker of a hand-held mixer or blender and puree.
6. Add beets and 3/4 cup cool water to the puree. Dissolve cornstarch in remaining 1/4 cup water.
7. Stir raisin and beet puree, dissolved cornstarch, sugar, and vanilla into cocoa mixture until at room temperature.
8. Quickly stir dry ingredients into cocoa mixture. Do not stir longer than needed to thoroughly mix the two.
9. Pour batter into prepared pan and smooth the surface.
10. Bake on top shelf for 35 to 40 minutes, or until a toothpick inserted in the middle of the cake comes out clean.
11. Let cake sit in pan for 10 to 15 minutes before removing to a wire rack. Cool completely before cutting.
